head.hi: The French Chargé d’affaires to the Secretary of State.

opener.seg: [Handed to the Secretary of State by the French chargé d’affaires, Mr. Thiébaut, October 4, 1900.—Translation.]

opener.dateline:
Embassy of the French Republic,
Washington, October 4, 1900.

div.p: Mr. Secretary of State: By order of my Government, I have the honor to bring to your excellency’s knowledge the following communication:

In sending their forces to China, the powers had as their first object
the rescue of their legations. Thanks to the united action and the valor of their troops, this end has been attained. The thing now to be done is to obtain from the Chinese Government, which has given to Prince Ching and Li Hung Chang full powers to negotiate and treat in its name, appropriate reparation for the past and substantial guaranties for the future.

If presented collectively by the representatives of the powers and backed up by the presence of the international troops, it seems to the Government of the Republic impossible that these conditions, so legitimate in themselves, should not speedily be accepted by the Chinese Government.
Be pleased to accept, Mr. Secretary of State, the renewed assurances of my highest consideration.
